zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Margaret Hepburn of Hailes於1424年出生在英國蘇格蘭哈丁頓史爾普雷斯頓吉可時，她的父親Sir Adam Hepburn of Hailes - Sheriff of Berwickshire42歲，母親Janet William Borthwick of Borthwick36歲。她於1445年1月16日與Sir Alexander Cunningham 1st Lord Kilmaus在蘇格蘭艾斯爾吉爾莫爾斯結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。她去世於1512年在英國蘇格蘭艾斯爾享年88歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Margaret Hepburn of Hailes于1424年出生在英国苏格兰哈丁頓史爾普雷斯頓吉可时，她的父亲Sir Adam Hepburn of Hailes - Sheriff of Berwickshire42岁，母亲Janet William Borthwick of Borthwick36岁。她于1445年1月16日与Sir Alexander Cunningham 1st Lord Kilmaus在苏格兰艾斯爾吉爾莫爾斯结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。她去世于1512年在英国苏格兰艾斯爾享年88岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hepburn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Durham) and Scottish: habitational name from Hepburn in Chillingham (Northumberland), Hebron (Northumberland), or Hebburn in Jarrow (Durham). The placenames probably all derive from Old English hēah ‘high’ + byrgen ‘burial place, tumulus’. Compare Hebron .

variants.name: Eborn
meanings: English (Middlesex, Buckinghamshire, and Oxfordshire): variant of Eburne, itself a possible variant of Hepburn . However, the placename Hepburn comes from northeastern England, and the H- spellings are a late development, which reinforces the suspicion that the name may have a different but unknown derivation.

variants.name: Hebron
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from Hebron (Northumberland), possibly from Old English hēah ‘high’ + byrgen ‘burial place, tumulus’. See also Hepburn .
Czech: from the Biblical placename (II Samuel 2: 1-3), said to mean ‘alliance’ in Hebrew.

variants.name: Heyburn
meanings: English: habitational name from a lost or unidentified place, probably in southern England, named with an unknown first element + Old English burna ‘spring, stream’.
Irish (Ulster): possibly a variant of Scottish   Hepburn  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Hailes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Hailes in Colinton (Midlothian), originally in East Lothian, named from the Middle English genitive or plural form of hall ‘hall’.
English: variant of   Hales   (see also   Hayles  ).

variants.name: Hales
meanings: English (widespread, especially in the southeast): from the genitive singular or nominative plural form of Old English halh ‘nook, recess’ (see Hale ).
Irish: when not of English origin, this may be a variant of   Healy   or   McHale  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Margaret Hepburn of Hailes was born in 1424, in Prestonkirk, Haddingtonshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Adam Hepburn of Hailes - Sheriff of Berwickshire, was 42 and her mother, Janet William Borthwick of Borthwick, was 36. She married Sir Alexander Cunningham 1st Lord Kilmaus on 16 January 1445, in Kilmaurs, Ayrshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. She died in 1512, in Ayrshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 88.

Elle a épousé Sir Alexander Cunningham 1st Lord Kilmaus le 16 janvier 1445, à Kilmaurs, Ayrshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Elle est décédée en 1512, à Ayrshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 88 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Hepburn of Hailes(이)가 1424년 영국 스코틀랜드 하딩토실르 프레스토커크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Adam Hepburn of Hailes - Sheriff of Berwickshire(은)는 42세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Janet William Borthwick of Borthwick(은)는 36세였다. 그녀는 1445년 1월 16일 스컷랜드 에이르실르 킬모르스에서 Sir Alexander Cunningham 1st Lord Kilmaus(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1512년 영국 스코틀랜드 에이르실르에서 88세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Margaret Hepburn of Hailes родилась в 1424 г. в местности престонкирк, хаддингтоншир, Шотландия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir Adam Hepburn of Hailes - Sheriff of Berwickshire было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Janet William Borthwick of Borthwick, 36. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Alexander Cunningham 1st Lord Kilmaus 16 января 1445 г. в местности килмаурс, аиршир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Она умерла в 1512 г. в местности аиршир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 88 лет.

Se casó con Sir Alexander Cunningham 1st Lord Kilmaus el 16 de enero de 1445, en Kilmaurs, Ayrshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió en 1512, en Ayrshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 88 años.
